channel.title: OviyamArt
channel.description: Returning to realistic pencil drawing after 10+ years away. Oviyam means drawing in Tamil. Hands only. No face.

item.title: The Weight of Shadows, and the Lift of Form
item.description:
This session was a battle of wills, where the simple act of adding shadows felt like lifting a heavy burden, until the form finally emerged.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: Beyond the Form: Why Emotions Challenge My Hand More Than Hands
item.description:
This session revealed that truly capturing human emotion in a realistic pencil sketch demands more than just skill; it requires an unexpected depth of feeling from the artist.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Unseen Work of Realism
item.description:
Sometimes, the deepest realism is found not in the subject itself, but in the subtle dance of shadows and light around it.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Weight of Expectations on a Blank Page
item.description:
Facing a complex reference after years away, I felt the familiar weight of self-doubt, but my hand found its way through the resistance.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: Unlearning to See: The Backward Eye
item.description:
Today, I faced a profound resistance attempting to draw an eye in an unconventional way, only to find a new path forward.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Weight of Eyelids: Bringing Life to a Gaze
item.description:
This post explores the unexpected difficulty of rendering eyelids and the quiet struggle to breathe life into a graphite eye.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Unblinking Gaze Confronting an Eye Without Eyelids
item.description:
Drawing an eye that lacked the familiar boundary of eyelids became an unexpected test of my patience and my understanding of form.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Unseen Layers: When My Lips Sketch Resisted Being 'Finished
item.description:
Returning to a 'finished' realistic pencil sketch of lips, I confronted the subtle resistance of my own perception, realizing true depth requires pushing past what first appears complete.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: Why My Lips Sketch Looked Broken on Shorts
item.description:
Why my lips sketch Short looked blurry and stretched — the horizontal HD to vertical crop mistake explained.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: Why My YouTube Short Stopped at 180 Views
item.description:
How a Pixabay audio track triggered a copyright claim and stopped my YouTube Short at 180 views.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: When the Outline Betrayed Me
item.description:
I thought this drawing was a complete failure, but pushing through the initial 'wrongness' revealed the profound power of realistic pencil shading.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Ear's Secret: Why My First Sketch Fell Flat
item.description:
This session was a humbling reminder that even the simplest forms demand true observation, revealing depth only after a visible struggle.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Unsettling Truth About Drawing Realistic Lips
item.description:
A candid look into the unexpected challenges of rendering realistic lips and what those struggles revealed about my artistic journey.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: Why Nose Are Harder Than Eyes
item.description:
A realistic pencil sketch study documenting the process of returning to graphite drawing after years away from art.

Watch on YouTube

item.title: The Kitchen Spoon Tripod How My Art Comeback Actually Started
item.description:
How I started my art comeback with just an A4 paper, a simple pencil, and a kitchen spoon as a tripod.

Watch on YouTube
